notes: v1.14.123 - UI sweep RULE A, pass 2 (deep-flow, completes 'one back per surface'). After .122 hid the legacy header chevron, the deploy DETAIL views still carried two backs with different semantics: #work-back (jump to grid) + the inline '‹ Back' pill (step up one level). Fix: on detail views, suppress the redundant grid-back and let the inline step-back be the single canonical back; the Command Center keeps #work-back (it has no inline pill). Mechanism: body.ops-detail class added by deploy_showDetail + deploy_showRackDetail, cleared by showMode (any mode switch) + deploy_showCommandCenter; CSS body.rd.ops-detail #pg-work:not(.wk-grid) #work-back{display:none!important}. nav_restore re-renders via these canonical fns, so the flag stays correct on history-back. Browser-verified full hierarchy at 412x915: Command Center = grid-back only; deployment + rack detail = inline step-back only (#work-back hidden); stepping back to CC restores #work-back; mode switch clears the flag; simple panels (Scan) keep their grid-back; zero console errors, no stuck state. Now EVERY redesign surface has exactly one back. 4 JS one-liners + 1 CSS rule; redesign-only (legacy has no body.rd and #work-back is display:none there) -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. HELD/offered separately: bump .rf-back-btn 40px->>=56px (Rule A tap-target) - visible size change, awaiting John's ok. node --check x3 + sw.js, brace gate, three-stamp lockstep. DEVICE-VERIFY: in a deployment/rack detail there's a single '‹ Back' that steps up one level; Command Center's back returns to the Work grid; ?legacy=1 unchanged.
